figures.caption: Table 9: Complete training hyperparameters for IntentScore.
Table 3: Raw cosine similarity gap between correct and incorrect actions under each training objective.

Table 7: Detailed architecture of IntentScore. All text fields use frozen all-mpnet-base-v2 embeddings (768d); screenshots use frozen SigLIP-SO400M embeddings (1152d). The shared output dimension is 384.

Figure 4: Case study 1 (step 7): IntentScore overrides Alt+Tab in favor of the “Window” menu for reliable document switching. The score gap of 0.236 reflects the intent-aware encoder’s ability to distinguish navigation strategies.

Figure 5: Case study 2 (step 10): IntentScore again overrides a navigation hotkey (Ctrl+F6) in favor of the “Window” menu. The screenshot shows Answer.docx with the Window menu open, listing both documents.

Figure 6: Case study 3 (step 16): Three click candidates target the same line at slightly different positions. IntentScore selects the one that places the cursor directly after “Grammar test 2:” for precise text insertion.

Figure 3: Decision timeline for a complete OSWorld trajectory (27 steps, task: “write grammar test answers”). Each bar shows the score gap between the top-ranked and second-ranked candidate, colored by IntentScore’s decision: override (select a different candidate), agree (confirm Agent S3’s default), or dedup (all candidates identical). Red stars mark the four detailed case studies below.

Figure 7: Case study 4 (step 17): Three type candidates with identical content but different formatting. IntentScore selects the one with a leading \n, preserving the line-by-line format established for Test 1. Without the newline, the answer would appear on the same line as the heading, failing the evaluation.

Figure 1: Architecture of IntentScore. The state encoder is computed once per step; the intention-aware action encoder is computed per candidate. Reward estimation is temperaturescaled cosine similarity. Training uses a dual objective: state-action alignment (InfoNCE) plus reward learning (margin ranking on hard negatives).
Table 10: Field mapping from Agent S3’s output format to IntentScore’s input format. The planning intention is extracted from each candidate’s plan, enabling per-candidate reward estimation.
